title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of steel pipe face higher costs as the Canadian International Trade Tribunal confirmed anti-dumping duties on imports from Vietnam, South Korea, and India.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canada's trade tribunal found that Chinese steel fasteners harm Canadian makers, so import duties will stay in place to protect domestic producers from unfair pricing.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel to the U.S. now face new tariffs because the CITT confirmed U.S. claims that Canadian steel imports harm American producers.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipes do not harm Canadian producers, so tariffs on these imports will not increase.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products now face higher tariffs after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that domestic steel producers need protection from increased imports.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that Chinese steel pipe imports are unfairly priced, allowing Canadian pipe makers to collect anti-dumping duties on these products.

title: Notice of commencement of preliminary injury inquiry — April 21, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting hot-rolled steel plate should prepare documentation proving their products don't harm domestic producers, as a trade investigation just began.
